content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Accept the `default_model` precedence regression amendment. Reject the final-review panel amendment because the cited run state is driven by an explicit live project override, while the current default-resolution code and tests already resolve unspecified final-review backends to Codex xhigh plus Claude Opus 4.6.

## Votes

- `fr-6-0736c40d`: Accept
  This is a real correctness regression. `EffectiveConfig::load` now always seeds an explicit Codex implementer selection, and `target_for_family()` prefers `role_models` before `base_backend.model`/`default_model`, so an explicit user override like `default_backend = "codex"` plus `default_model = "override-model"` no longer changes the implementer or the default Codex final-review reviewer. Compiled role defaults should not silently outrank the user-facing `default_model` override path.
- `fr-6-013a5281`: Reject
  The conclusion is not supported by the actual resolution source. Unspecified defaults in source are already `final_review.backends = [codex, claude]`, with tests asserting Codex `gpt-5.4-xhigh` plus Claude `claude-opus-4-6`; the cited `run.json` and `journal.ndjson` entries instead match the explicit live project override in `.git/ralph-burning-live/projects/utm/config.toml` (`final_review.backends = ["codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh", "codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh"]`). Changing default resolution on that basis would be the wrong fix and would break legitimate project-specific overrides.
